channel.title: Pulling Threads
channel.description: Mostly shit talking, occasionally to great people...
channel.author: Alex Cleary
channel.itunes:author: Alex Cleary
channel.itunes:summary: Mostly shit talking, occasionally to great people...

item.title: The Renovation That Broke Me
item.description:
Alex spent an entire week off work trying to build a deck. What he found underground nearly sent him over the edge. Buried rocks the size of a coffin, mystery pipes, failed inspections, and a $1,200 hole-digging bill with almost nothing to show for it. This is the most honest renovation meltdown you'll ever hear.

In this episode:
- The full deck disaster saga, from Kanga loaders to jackhammers to total surrender
- Troy's post-race comedown and what it feels like when the cheering stops
- Why running in the rain alone after a big race is where the real work begins
- The official launch of the Pulling Threads Book Club and the first book pick
- Top 3 best smells of all time, including one that will divide every listener
- The evolutionary reason your armpits stink and whether clipping them actually helps
- Why one night camping fixed more stress than a week of grinding ever could

Timestamps:
(00:14) Welcome back and Troy's post-race comedown
(01:01) The emotional low after finishing your first ultra
(03:18) Ultra runner status and run inflation
(05:06) Alex's week of severe unhappiness begins
(06:46) The deck project prep, pallets of concrete and $6K of composite decking
(10:39) First hole hits a massive buried rock
(17:09) Digging out a mystery rock the size of a 12-year-old's burial plot
(21:06) Second row of holes hits a steel pipe underground
(25:25) $1,200 in Kanga hire and almost no holes to show for it
(28:49) Calling off the deck and reverse logistics nightmare
(33:27) Launching the Pulling Threads Book Club
(38:10) What makes a book worth reading on the pod
(42:59) Top 3 best smells, KFC outside the building
(47:37) The smell of rain and the science of petrichor
(50:00) Petrol at the go-kart track
(51:31) Troy's final pick and the evolutionary purpose of armpit hair

Full episode out now on Spotify and Apple Podcasts.
Follow the pod on Instagram: @pullingthreads

Pick up Blaine & Amber's book

item.itunes:summary:
Alex spent an entire week off work trying to build a deck. What he found underground nearly sent him over the edge. Buried rocks the size of a coffin, mystery pipes, failed inspections, and a $1,200 hole-digging bill with almost nothing to show for it. This is the most honest renovation meltdown you'll ever hear.

In this episode:
- The full deck disaster saga, from Kanga loaders to jackhammers to total surrender
- Troy's post-race comedown and what it feels like when the cheering stops
- Why running in the rain alone after a big race is where the real work begins
- The official launch of the Pulling Threads Book Club and the first book pick
- Top 3 best smells of all time, including one that will divide every listener
- The evolutionary reason your armpits stink and whether clipping them actually helps
- Why one night camping fixed more stress than a week of grinding ever could

Timestamps:
(00:14) Welcome back and Troy's post-race comedown
(01:01) The emotional low after finishing your first ultra
(03:18) Ultra runner status and run inflation
(05:06) Alex's week of severe unhappiness begins
(06:46) The deck project prep, pallets of concrete and $6K of composite decking

(25:25) $1,200 in Kanga hire and almost no holes to show for it
(28:49) Calling off the deck and reverse logistics nightmare
(33:27) Launching the Pulling Threads Book Club
(38:10) What makes a book worth reading on the pod
(42:59) Top 3 best smells, KFC outside the building
(47:37) The smell of rain and the science of petrichor
(50:00) Petrol at the go-kart track
(51:31) Troy's final pick and the evolutionary purpose of armpit hair

Full episode out now on Spotify and Apple Podcasts.
Follow the pod on Instagram: @pullingthreads

Pick up Blaine & Amber's book

item.title: The Toxic Masculinity Test That Exposed Us Both
item.description:
Alex and Troy take an online toxic masculinity test live and compare their results. It goes exactly how you'd expect. One brother scores more toxic than average, the other less, and Troy's wife somehow outscores them both on deceitful arrogance. Before that, the boys rip through their signs of getting old, from war history podcasts to Crocs and socks to refusing to flush the toilet. Plus a tradie from Townsville who invoices builders for premium toilet paper and a deep dive into how tech companies have been lying to your face for years.

In this episode:
- Wild Ball race predictions and Troy's secret Strava rival named Jared
- Signs you're getting old including being obsessed with war history and refusing to go to 9pm movies
- Damo from Townsville's genius toilet paper invoicing scheme
- How phone companies swap glass types every year to fake progress
- The toxic masculinity test results and the questions that had them stumped
- Troy's wife scoring 47% on deceitful arrogance and what that says about board game night
- The leading question about homosexuals as role models and why the test methodology is cooked
- Park run recap and why negative splits made Troy feel like a king

Timestamps:
(00:00) Intro and Wild Ball race predictions
(04:17) Troy's first park run and battling kids on scooters
(08:37) Alex's prediction for Troy and the secret competitor Jared
(11:30) Signs you're getting old list
(17:49) Becoming your dad about lights, water, and heating
(23:34) Crocs and socks conversion story
(28:52) Stingy or smart: Damo's toilet paper invoice hustle
(35:47) How they get you: tech companies faking progress
(42:05) The toxic masculinity test begins
(48:18) Deceitful arrogance and trait anger results
(55:04) Heteronormativity and homophobia scores
(58:14) Final toxic masculinity scores and Troy's wife's results

Full episode out now on Spotify and Apple Podcasts.
Follow the pod on Instagram: @pullingthreads


Do your Toxic Masculinity test here
